definition: Polymorphism means one interface or superclass reference can refer to different subclass objects, and the method implementation runs according to the runtime object.
deepExplanation: The reference type controls which methods are legal to call. The actual object type controls which overridden method body runs. This lets client code depend on a stable general type while new subclasses provide new behavior.

codeExamples.title: Same call, different behavior

diagrams.caption: The call expression is the same, but the runtime object selects the method body.

commonMistakes: Expecting static methods to dispatch dynamically.
practiceQuestions: If Shape s = new Rectangle(3, 4), which area method runs?
